data.body: Consideration (adversarial review): [Consumer Impact] `validate.sh --ci` historically runs DSO plugin self-tests (skill-ref checks, contract-schema checks, hook lint) that are meaningful only when `plugins/dso/` is present in the repo. Host projects consume DSO via the marketplace, not as source. Without scoping, validate-ci becomes either vacuously passing or always failing on host projects. Verify the d025-87d4 'commands resolve without DSO plugin in CI runner' constraint applies specifically to the validate-ci job; document which subset of validate.sh checks are meaningful in host-project CI vs. plugin-repo CI.
